question: Rajiv Gandhi Khel Ratna Award was conferred ______ Mary Kom, a six-time world champion in boxing, recently in a ceremony ______ the Rashtrapati Bhawan (the President's official residence) in New Delhi.
Despite a string of poor performances, the chances of K. L. Rahul's selection in the team are ______.

Select the word that fits the analogy:
Cover: Uncover :: Associate : ______

Hit by floods, the kharif (summer sown) crops in various parts of the country have been affected. Officials believe that the loss in production of the kharif crops can be recovered in the output of the rabi (winter sown) crops so that the country can achieve its food-grain production target of 291 million tons in the crop year 2019-20 (July-June). They are hopeful that good rains in July-August will help the soil retain moisture for a longer period, helping winter sown crops such as wheat and pulses during the November-February period.

Which of the following statements can be inferred from the given passage?

options.A: Officials declared that the food-grain production target will be met due to good rains.
options.B: Officials want the food-grain production target to be met by the November-February period.
options.C: Officials feel that the food-grain production target cannot be met due to floods.
options.D: Officials hope that the food-grain production target will be met due to a good rabi produce.

question: The difference between the sum of the first $2n$ natural numbers and the sum of the first $n$ odd natural numbers is ______

Repo rate is the rate at which Reserve Bank of India (RBI) lends commercial banks, and reverse repo rate is the rate at which RBI borrows money from commercial banks.

Which of the following statements can be inferred from the above passage?

options.A: Decrease in repo rate will increase cost of borrowing and decrease lending by commercial banks.
options.B: Increase in repo rate will decrease cost of borrowing and increase lending by commercial banks.
options.C: Increase in repo rate will decrease cost of borrowing and decrease lending by commercial banks.
options.D: Decrease in repo rate will decrease cost of borrowing and increase lending by commercial banks.

question:
P, Q, R, S, T, U, V, and W are seated around a circular table.
I. S is seated opposite to W.
II. U is seated at the second place to the right of R.
III. T is seated at the third place to the left of R.
IV. V is a neighbour of S.

Which of the following must be true?

options.A: P is a neighbour of R.
options.B: Q is a neighbour of R.
options.C: P is not seated opposite to Q.
options.D: R is the left neighbour of S.

question: The distance between Delhi and Agra is 233 km. A car P started travelling from Delhi to Agra and another car Q started from Agra to Delhi along the same road 1 hour after the car P started. The two cars crossed each other 75 minutes after the car Q started. Both cars were travelling at constant speed. The speed of car P was 10 km/hr more than the speed of car Q. How many kilometers the car Q had travelled when the cars crossed each other?
For a matrix $M = [m_{ij}]$; $i,j = 1,2,3,4$, the diagonal elements are all zero and $m_{ij} = -m_{ji}$. The minimum number of elements required to fully specify the matrix is ______

The profit shares of two companies P and Q are shown in the figure. If the two companies have invested a fixed and equal amount every year, then the ratio of the total revenue of company P to the total revenue of company Q, during 2013 - 2018 is ______.
```
(Image shows a bar chart titled 'Profit percentage' vs 'Year' for Company P and Company Q from 2013 to 2018.
Profit percentages are approximately:
2013: P=10%, Q=15%
2014: P=20%, Q=25%
2015: P=35%, Q=45%
2016: P=45%, Q=55%
2017: P=55%, Q=65%
2018: P=40%, Q=60%)
```

As per URDPFI Guidelines 2015, Government of India, choose the correct hierarchy of plans from higher to lower order.

options.A: Perspective plan, Development plan, Regional plan, Zonal plan
options.B: Perspective plan, Regional plan, Development plan, Zonal plan
options.C: Regional plan, Perspective plan, Development plan, Zonal plan
options.D: Zonal plan, Development plan, Regional plan, Perspective plan

In India, the Constitution (Seventy Fourth Amendment) Act, 1992, delegates powers to institutions forming the third tier of government, which are ______

options.A: Municipal Corporation, Municipality and Nagar Panchayat
options.B: Development Authority, Municipal Corporation and Municipality
options.C: Improvement Trust, Nagar Panchayat and Panchayat
options.D: Development Authority, Improvement Trust and Panchayat

Match the images of gardens in Group I with their names in Group II
```
Group I
(P) Image of a large, rectangular, formal garden with a central axis, radiating paths, and geometric planting beds. It features a large central water body and symmetrical layout.
(Q) Image of a terraced garden with a central water channel, fountains, and a large lake at the lowest level. The layout is linear and symmetrical along the central axis.
(R) Image of a highly intricate formal garden with elaborate parterres de broderie, fountains, and geometric patterns, viewed from above.
(S) Image of a traditional Japanese garden with a large pond, islands, bridges, and carefully placed rocks and trees, designed for contemplation.

Group II
(1) Central Park, New York
(2) Versailles, Paris
(3) Nishat Bagh, Srinagar
(4) Katsura Imperial Garden, Kyoto
(5) Alhambra Moorish Garden, Granada
```

Based on the psychrometric chart given below, match the vector in Group I with the respective process in Group II
```
(Image shows a psychrometric chart with Relative Humidity on the y-axis (implicitly Humidity Ratio) and Dry bulb Temperature (Deg. C) on the x-axis. Four vectors are drawn:
V1: Horizontal arrow pointing right (increasing temperature, constant humidity ratio).
V2: Arrow pointing up-right (increasing temperature, increasing humidity ratio).
V3: Arrow pointing up-left (decreasing temperature, increasing humidity ratio).
V4: Horizontal arrow pointing left (decreasing temperature, constant humidity ratio).)

Group I
(P) V1
(Q) V2
(R) V3
(S) V4

Group II
(1) Heating and humidification

(3) Heating and dehumidification
```


Match the characteristics in Group I with the type of settlements in Group II as given in URDPFI Guidelines 2015, Government of India
```
Group I
(P) Zones of transition from rural to urban land uses located between the outer limits of urban and regional centres and rural environment
(Q) Towns having potential for investment and development; identified on the basis of their inter-aerial relationship with the regional nodal centre
(R) Settlements that are growing sub-nodal centres but located out of the direct functionally linked areas of the growth node / nodal centre in the region
(S) Located near or within reasonable distance, well connected by transportation route of the growth node or metropolitan city and dependent on growth node largely for employment

Group II
(1) Counter-Magnets
(2) Satellite Towns
(3) Peri-Urban Areas
(4) Priority Towns
(5) Statutory Towns
```

```
Type | Area (m²) | Run-off coefficient
---|---|---|---
Apartment Blocks | 1250 | 0.80
Central Play Area | 150 | 0.60
Other Permeable Areas | 200 | 0.70
Other Impermeable Areas | 400 | 0.90
```
The required depth of the detention pond (in mm) is ______

A developer would like to select a residential plot of $3000 m^2$ for group housing in a city. Different options with varying development controls are given. In every group housing plot, 15% of the Floor Area Ratio (FAR) over and above the maximum permissible FAR has to be utilized for Economically Weaker Section (EWS) units.
```
Area | Ground Coverage (%) | FAR
---|---|---|---
1 | 30 | 1.5
2 | 20 | 2.0
3 | 40 | 2.0
4 | 15 | 3.0
```
